name: Individual Apricot Charlottes
recipeIngredient: 12 tablespoons (1 1/2 sticks) unsalted butter, melted and cooled
2 pounds firm apricots, halved, pitted and sliced
recipeInstructions:
To line the molds (8, 4 to 6-ounce ramekins):
1. Slice the bread very thinly.
2. Cut out 8 rounds of bread, the size of the inside bottoms of the molds.
3. Dip each round in butter and place in molds, buttered side down.
4. Line sides of molds with 3 to 4 overlapping rectangles of bread, cut to fit, dipping each in butter before placing inside molds.
5. Set aside 8 pieces of bread to cover the filling after it is added to the molds.
6. Place the molds on a jelly roll pan.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees and set rack to middle level.
For the filling:
1. Prepare apricots and set aside.
2. Melt butter in a shallow sauté pan.
3. Add apricots, cooking until they begin to sizzle.
4. Add sugar and continue cooking another 5 minutes, until juices are thickened and syrupy.
5. Add rum and allow it to boil out.
6. Add crumbs and stir in.
Fill molds with apricot mixture, then cover with reserved bread, buttered side out.
1. Bake for 30 minutes, or until the visible bread is very well toasted.
2. Serve immediately with whipped cream or apricot sauce.
